t_ch: 广告牌满怀期待地晃了晃。画面上的女子确实气质独特，很有魅力。
t_en: The billboard sways with anticipation. The young girl on the screen does indeed seem to have a unique quality. She is very charming.

t_ch: 没见过。
t_en: ...Didn't see her.

t_ch: 好吧…看来我还要继续等下去……
t_en: Alright... I guess I'll have to keep searching...

t_ch: 如果您以后在某处遇见她，请务必替我递上一句话：「有只广告牌在中央车站前等你，它会一直等下去的！」
t_en: If you happen to meet her somewhere, please tell her one thing for me: "There is a billboard in front of the Golden Central Station waiting for you. It will always be waiting for you!"

t_ch: 我会帮你留意。
t_en: I will let her know.

t_ch: 太好了，{F#小姐}{M#先生}——您真是个大好人！
t_en: Thank you, {F#miss}{M#sir}... You truly are a kind person!

t_ch: 如果您以后在某处遇见她，请务必替我递上一句话：「有只广告牌在中央车站前等你，它会一直等下去的！」
t_en: If you happen to meet her somewhere, please tell her one thing for me: "There is a billboard in front of the Golden Central Station waiting for you. It will always be waiting for you!"

t_ch: 再聊聊你的爱人吧。
t_en: Tell me more about this love of yours.

t_ch: 当然可以，您想问什么？
t_en: Of course! What do you want to know?

t_ch: 你们是怎么相爱的？
t_en: How did you fall in love?

t_ch: 事实上，那是我们的第一次相遇…
t_en: To tell you the truth, it was the first time we met...

t_ch: 那天中央车站的人很多，但我一眼就在人群中看见了她。她越走越近，在一众广告牌中准确无误地走到我面前。
t_en: There were many people in the Golden Central Station that day, but my eyes immediately picked her out of the crowd. She walked closer and closer and, amongst all of the many billboards around, she came and stood in front of me.

t_ch: 啊，一切用于形容美好的词汇都不足以形容那个瞬间——就像是宾客喝多了苏乐达，晕晕乎乎，但无比快乐！
t_en: Ah, any words that describe beauty would fail to capture the perfection of that moment... I was like a guest that had overdone it on SoulGlad, dizzy, but that's the happiest moment I had ever had!

t_ch: 只可惜她离开得太快了，我还没来得及和她问好。为了不错过下一次相遇，我决定变成{RUBY_B#她的样子}她的专属广告牌{RUBY_E#}！
t_en: Unfortunately, she left so quickly that I didn't have time to greet her. I decided that I would become a billboard {RUBY_B#In Her Image}solely dedicated to her{RUBY_E#}!

t_ch: 她的名字是？
t_en: What's her name?

t_ch: 广告牌僵住了，它晃了晃，差点翻倒在地。
t_en: The billboard froze in place. Swaying, it almost fell to the ground.

t_ch: 呀！我还不知道她的名字呢！
t_en: Oh no! I don't even know her name yet!

t_ch: 毕竟我们只见过一次——但没关系，我记下她的样子，一定能再找到她！
t_en: We've only met once, after all... But it's okay. I remember what she looked like. I know I can find her again!

t_ch: 你之前是干什么的？
t_en: What did you do before this?

t_ch: 我曾是{RUBY_B#「蓝调的时刻」}第九梦境{RUBY_E#}的推销员，专门负责在车站前宣传它们的婚恋服务。
t_en: I used to be a salesman for {RUBY_B#Blue Hour}the ninth Dreamscape{RUBY_E#}. I was personally responsible for promoting their marriage services in front of the Golden Central Station.

t_ch: 但在与命中注定的她相遇后，我才明白那些信誓旦旦的广告词到底有多虚伪！
t_en: But, after I met my true love, I suddenly realized how much of a sham all of the promises these advertisements make are!

t_ch: 为了追求真正的爱情——我决定成为{RUBY_B#她的样子}她的专属广告牌{RUBY_E#}！
t_en: In order to find my true love... I decided that I would become a billboard {RUBY_B#In Her Image}solely dedicated to her{RUBY_E#}!

t_ch: 就这样吧，拜拜。
t_en: That's all. Bye-bye.

t_ch: 再见了，{F#美丽的小姐}{M#英俊的先生}，祝您旅途愉快——如果您以后在某处遇见她，请务必替我递上一句话：「有只广告牌在中央车站前等你，它会一直等下去的！」
t_en: Farewell, {F#beautiful lady}{M#handsome gentleman}, I wish you a pleasant journey... If you happen to meet her somewhere, please tell her one thing for me: "There is a billboard in front of the Golden Central Station waiting for you. It will always be waiting for you!"

t_ch: 就这样吧，拜拜。
t_en: That's all. Bye-bye.

t_ch: 再见了，{F#美丽的小姐}{M#英俊的先生}，祝您旅途愉快——如果您以后在某处遇见她，请务必替我递上一句话：「有只广告牌在中央车站前等你，它会一直等下去的！」
t_en: Farewell, {F#beautiful lady}{M#handsome gentleman}, I wish you a pleasant journey... If you happen to meet her somewhere, please tell her one thing for me: "There is a billboard in front of the Golden Central Station waiting for you. It will always be waiting for you!"

t_ch: 广告牌跳起了一首滑稽的交际舞。
t_en: The billboard is performing a hilarious ballroom dance.

t_ch: 一二三、二二三……哦{F#小姐}{M#先生}！我正在练习呢！
t_en: 1, 2, 3, 2, 2, 3... Oh, {F#miss}{M#sir}! I am in the middle of practice!

t_ch: 之前常有人在广场边上做这种动作，邀请恋人一起跳舞——他们看起来可真幸福！
t_en: There always used to be people in the square that would move like this, inviting their lovers to dance... They looked so happy!

t_ch: 或许，我是说或许…嘿嘿，下次见面的时候，她也愿意和我共舞一曲吗？
t_en: Maybe, just maybe... Hehe... Next time we meet, she would be willing to dance with me?

t_ch: 广告牌停下动作，像是从酣醉中猛然清醒。
t_en: The billboard stops moving, as if suddenly awakening from a drunken haze.

t_ch: 哦…不……我太天真了。
t_en: Oh... No... I'm just being naïve.

t_ch: 看似浪漫的对视，没有告别的离去…那不叫「相爱」，我不过是她在梦中遇到的一只随处可见的广告牌罢了。
t_en: That look we shared that seemed so romantic, that parting without a farewell... That is not "falling in love." I'm just a billboard that she can see anywhere in her dream.

t_ch: 或许她早就离开匹诺康尼了，而我却还在期待毫无意义的再会…哦，太傻了。
t_en: Maybe she already left Penacony long ago, and I am still holding onto hope for a meaningless reunion... Ah, I'm such a fool...

t_ch: 你该继续等下去。
t_en: You should keep waiting.

t_ch: 或许吧。
t_en: Perhaps.

t_ch: 毕竟除了等待，我也没有别的事可做了。
t_en: There's nothing for me to do but wait, after all.

t_ch: 广告牌不再说话，它看起来和其他广告牌别无两样了。
t_en: The billboard has stopped talking. It looks just the same as all the other billboards around here.

t_ch: 你可以去别的地方找她。
t_en: You could go somewhere else to search for her.

t_ch: ……我该主动去其他时刻找她？
t_en: ...I should proactively go to the other Moments to find her?

t_ch: 你说的有道理…没人规定广告牌不能搭乘「球笼」…或许我可以试着潜入车站……
t_en: You're right... There are no rules stating that billboards can't take the Spheroid... Maybe I could try sneaking into the station...

t_ch: 广告牌不再说话，似乎陷入了沉思。
t_en: The billboard has stopped talking. It seems to have fallen into deep thought.

t_ch: 广告牌像是被自己先前亢奋的声音噎住，突然大哭起来。
t_en: The billboard seems to become choked up by its previous outburst of excitement and suddenly bursts into tears.

t_ch: 我早就知道原地等待没有结果，其实我就是个胆小鬼…呜呜…宁愿闷头扎在车站门口，等一个虚无缥缈的好结局……
t_en: I always knew that just standing here waiting in one place would never work. I'm honestly just a coward... *wails*... I'd rather stand here in front of the station entrance and wait for an extremely unlikely happy ending...

t_ch: 如果再也没法与她见面，或者，或者她讨厌我，不接受我的感情怎么办？
t_en: What if I never get to see her again? What if she hates me and doesn't accept my love?

t_ch: 我、我实在太害怕了……
t_en: I... I'm just so scared...

t_ch: ……对不起{F#小姐}{M#先生}，是我失态了。来点苏乐达吧，这能让咱们两个都好受点……
t_en: ...I'm sorry, {F#ma'am}{M#sir}. I lost my composure there. Let's have some SoulGlad. That will perk both of us up a bit...

t_ch: 广告牌抹了抹并不存在的眼泪，印在它身体上的女子依然端庄地笑着。
t_en: The billboard wipes away its non-existent tears as the woman printed on its body continues to smile with a dignified air.

t_ch: 我早就知道原地等待没有结果，其实我就是个胆小鬼…呜呜…宁愿闷头扎在车站门口，等一个虚无缥缈的好结局……
t_en: I always knew that just standing here waiting in one place would never work. I'm honestly just a coward... *wails*... I'd rather stand here in front of the station entrance and wait for an extremely unlikely happy ending...

t_ch: 如果再也没法与她见面，或者，或者她讨厌我，不接受我的感情怎么办？
t_en: What if I never get to see her again? What if she hates me and doesn't accept my love?

t_ch: 我、我实在太害怕了……
t_en: I... I'm just so scared...

t_ch: 你，你，你！还有你！你们都是骗子！
t_en: You, you, and you too! You're all liars!

t_ch: 广告牌指着街上路过的小情侣骂骂咧咧。
t_en: The billboard points at couples passing by in the street and curses them.

t_ch: 什么蜜月，什么忠诚，什么真挚不渝！假的广告，假的爱情！
t_en: So much for "honeymoons," "loyalty," and "true love"! It's all false advertising! Fake love!

t_ch: 上一秒嘴里还是甜言蜜语，下一秒转头就拉起别人的手——广告牌看得一清二楚！假的，全都是假的！！
t_en: One minute you're whispering sweet nothings and the next, you're holding someone else's hand... This billboard sees everything clearly! It's fake! It's all fake!!!

t_ch: 但…假的也行啊，让我再见一次她吧。
t_en: But... Even if it's fake, I still wish that I could meet her just one more time.

t_ch: 广告牌不说话了。一张传单从它身上掉下来。
t_en: The billboard has stopped talking. A leaflet falls from its body to the ground.

t_ch: 什么蜜月，什么忠诚，什么真挚不渝！假的广告，假的爱情！
t_en: So much for "honeymoons," "loyalty," and "true love"! It's all false advertising! Fake love!

t_ch: 上一秒嘴里还是甜言蜜语，下一秒转头就拉起别人的手——广告牌看得一清二楚！假的，全都是假的！！
t_en: One minute you're whispering sweet nothings and the next, you're holding someone else's hand... This billboard sees everything clearly! It's fake! It's all fake!!!

t_ch: 但…假的也行啊，让我再见一次她吧。
t_en: But... Even if it's fake, I still wish that I could meet her just one more time.

t_ch: 动画家、梦境大亨、匹诺康尼之父…这座城市中的每个人都听说过他，这座城市的每处细节也都与他有关。
t_en: A great animator, a Dreamscape tycoon, father of Penacony... Everyone in this city has heard his name, and every single detail of this city is intertwined with him.

t_ch: 但似乎没人真正见过他的样子——说到底，<color=#dbc291ff>「钟表匠」</color>到底是什么人呢？
t_en: But it seems no one has ever seen him before... Just what kind of person is this <color=#dbc291ff>"Watchmaker"</color>?

t_ch: 听说是他创建了匹诺康尼。
t_en: I hear that he created Penacony.

t_ch: 这确实是大多数人的印象。与「钟表匠」相关的记录虽少，但他的成就举世皆知，可以说是当下匹诺康尼盛世的最大功臣。
t_en: That's the impression that most people have. There are very few records on the Watchmaker, but his achievements are known by all. You could say that Penacony's prosperity is mostly thanks to him.

t_ch: 这让我感到些许疑惑——如此伟大的「工匠」，在匹诺康尼里却见不到他的作品？
t_en: I just find this kind of confusing... Why are the works of such a great "craftsman" nowhere to be seen in Penacony?

t_ch: 也许「钟表匠」只是美称？
t_en: Perhaps "Watchmaker" is just a title?

t_ch: 或许如此，但我仍好奇这背后的故事。真希望能与这位伟大的同僚亲自见上一面。
t_en: Perhaps, but I still want to know the story behind this. I really wish I could meet this great fellow craftsman of mine in person.

t_ch: 同僚？
t_en: Fellow craftsman?

t_ch: 我也很好奇！
t_en: I'm curious, too!

t_ch: 正是受好奇心驱使，我才决定千里迢迢来到匹诺康尼，真希望能与这位伟大的同僚亲自见上一面。
t_en: It was curiosity that spurred me to make the long journey to Penacony. I truly wish that I could meet this great fellow craftsman in person.

t_ch: 同僚？
t_en: Fellow craftsman?

t_ch: 是他邀请我们来到这场盛会。
t_en: It was he who invited us to this celebration.

t_ch: 嗯？您指的是「谐乐大典」吗？那是由家族每纪定期主办的庆典，与「钟表匠」本人无关。
t_en: Hm? You mean the Charmony Festival? That is a celebration that The Family arranges regularly, and it has nothing to do with the Watchmaker.

t_ch: 虽然「钟表匠」的成就举世皆知，但匹诺康尼中有关他真容的记录极少。这让我感到些许疑惑：如此伟大的「工匠」，在匹诺康尼里却见不到他的作品？
t_en: Though the achievements of the Watchmaker are known by all, there are very few records of his true appearance in Penacony. I just find this kind of confusing... Why are the works of such a great "craftsman" nowhere to be seen in Penacony?

t_ch: 真希望能与这位伟大的同僚亲自见上一面。
t_en: I truly wish that I could meet this great fellow craftsman in person.

t_ch: 同僚？
t_en: Fellow craftsman?

t_ch: 失礼，刚刚没来得及自我介绍——鄙人来自螺丝星附近的「哈利伯顿钟表工坊」，是一位钟表匠人。
t_en: My apologies. I got carried away and never introduced myself... I am a watchmaker from the Haliburton Watch Workshop near Planet Screwllum.

t_ch: 在过去的一个琥珀纪里，众多富豪名流都曾雇我为他们制作钟表。
t_en: In the last Amber Era, many of the rich and famous would employ me to create timepieces for them.

t_ch: 他们渴望拥有属于个人的奇迹，我便在制造过程中加入与众不同的原料：稀世罕见的珠宝；陨星的碎片；甚至无机生命本身。
t_en: They desired a miracle that was all their own, so I began to introduce unusual materials into the crafting process: Rare gemstones, meteorite fragments, and even inorganic life itself.

t_ch: 但我却总觉得哪里不足——我此生所求无非是掌握寰宇间最极致的制表工艺，并打造出一枚世间无二的「钟表」！
t_en: However, I felt that something was missing... All I desire in this life is to master the most exquisite craftsmanship techniques and create a "timepiece" like no other!

t_ch: 所以，此行受邀来到匹诺康尼，正是想与此地最著名的工匠交流钟表技艺。我敢断定，这银河间一定有我未曾染指的新技术。
t_en: So, I accepted this invitation to come to Penacony because I wish to discuss the art of making timepieces with this land's most famous craftsman. I am certain that there must be new techniques in the cosmos that I have never been exposed to.

t_ch: 只可惜这位「钟表匠」一直不肯露面…我该怎么做才能见到他呢？
t_en: It is just a shame that this Watchmaker is not willing to show his face... How am I supposed to meet with him?

t_ch: 我们聊聊吧？
t_en: Let's have a talk.

t_ch: 当然可以。您是掌握了什么和「钟表匠」有关的消息了吗？
t_en: Of course. Have you gained some information on the Watchmaker?

t_ch: 聊聊「钟表匠」
t_en: Let's talk about the Watchmaker.

t_ch: 动画家、梦境大亨、匹诺康尼之父…虽然「钟表匠」的成就举世皆知，但匹诺康尼中有关他真容的记录极少。
t_en: A great animator, a Dreamscape tycoon, father of Penacony... Though the achievements of the Watchmaker are known by all, there are very few records of his true appearance in Penacony.

t_ch: 他是如此伟大的「工匠」，但我甚至在匹诺康尼里却见不到他的作品。
t_en: He is such a great "craftsman," yet I cannot see a single one of his works in Penacony.

t_ch: 您对此有什么了解吗？
t_en: Do you know why this might be?

t_ch: （「钟表匠」最重要的作品…或许就在我们身边……）
t_en: (The Watchmaker's most important work... may be right beside us)

t_ch: 钟表小子动画。
t_en: Clockie the cartoon.

t_ch: 听起来似乎和钟表还有些联系。谢谢您，我会去看看的。
t_en: That sounds related to clocks and watches. Thank you. I'll go and check it out.

t_ch: 克劳克影视乐园。
t_en: Clock Studios Theme Park.

t_ch: 听起来似乎和钟表没什么联系…但还是谢谢您，我会抽空去看看的。
t_en: That doesn't sound related to clocks... But thank you. I will make time to check it out.

t_ch: 匹诺康尼的「十二时分」。
t_en: The "Twelve Hours" of Penacony.

t_ch: …原来如此。「十二时分」的梦境构成表盘，而串联起十二个梦境的球笼成为在表盘上跃动的指针——或许，匹诺康尼本身即为「钟表匠」所造的最精密、最宏大的作品！
t_en: ...I see. The Dreamscape of the "Twelve Hours" form a clock face, and the Spheroids connect the twelve Dreamscapes as the clock hands dance between them... Perhaps Penacony itself is the Watchmaker's most meticulous and magnificent work!

t_ch: 话虽如此，它的制作技法早已脱离了钟表本身的概念…又或者，钟表本身也不该被局限在某个固定模式中。我所追求的极致工艺亦是如此。
t_en: Saying that, the way it is crafted had long since exceeded the concept of "clock"... Or perhaps "clocks" should never have been restricted to a set style. This is exactly the exquisite craftsmanship I was searching for!

t_ch: 无论如何——谢谢您，您的这番话对我意义非凡。
t_en: Either way... Thank you. This conversation has meant a lot to me.

t_ch: 聊聊你自己。
t_en: Tell me about yourself.

t_ch: 关于你最满意的作品。
t_en: Tell me about the work you're most proud of.

t_ch: 真是个尖锐的问题，{F#小姐}{M#先生}。作为工匠的哈利伯顿会告诉您：「我最满意的作品永远是下一个」，但从更私人的角度来说…
t_en: A sharp question, {F#ma'am}{M#sir}. I, Haliburton, will always say as a craftsman: "My next work will be my proudest," but if you're asking for my personal opinion...

t_ch: 哈利伯顿敲了敲自己的胸口，那里正传来嘀嗒嘀嗒的计时声。
t_en: Haliburton knocks on his chest, where you hear a ticking noise.

t_ch: 以钟声模拟心跳，齿轮重塑生命的动力，我确实造出了一份足够令自己满意的作品——一颗<color=#dbc291ff>无机生命的心脏</color>。
t_en: By using the sound of a clock to mimic a heartbeat and gears to reshape the power of life, I did indeed create one work that has made me satisfied and proud... An <color=#dbc291ff>inorganic heart</color>.

t_ch: 当然，它还算不上稳定，我还在思考改善方案。
t_en: Of course, it is still not entirely stable. I am still thinking of a plan to improve it.

t_ch: 我一直抱有希望…希望「钟表匠」所有的的技术能给我带来新灵感。
t_en: I have always held hope... Hope that the skills of the Watchmaker could bring me new inspiration.

t_ch: 关于你的制表工艺。
t_en: Tell me about your watchmaking.

t_ch: 细碎的星震碎片可以成为怀表表盘的点缀，而泯灭帮的淬火可以被用来熔炼座钟的摆锤。根据不同材料的性质，我会将它们打造成最为合适的配件。
t_en: Fine fragments of starquake can embellish watch faces and the Annihilation Gang's tempering fire can be used to forge a grandfather clock's pendulum. Depending on their properties, I use different materials to make the most fitting parts of a clock.

t_ch: 最顶尖的钟表匠人不会为自己设下限制，任何材料都可以成为钟表制作的原料。
t_en: The greatest watchmakers would never set limits on themselves. Anything can become material for watchmaking.

t_ch: 同时，这也是项极其理性的工作——一切需要万分小心。上一任哈利伯顿，也就是我的师父，就被毁于将星核嵌入表身时产生的爆炸波中。
t_en: At the same time, it is a job that requires a great amount of rationalism... Everything must be done with great caution. The previous Haliburton before me, my tutor, was destroyed in an explosion when he embedded a Stellaron into a watch face.

t_ch: 十分抱歉…
t_en: I'm so sorry...

t_ch: 没关系，这也是他预想中的结果。
t_en: It's fine. This was the result that he had predicted.

t_ch: 我们根据遗嘱取用了他身体中的零件，重新拼合成了响彻整个星系的「哈利伯顿刻心自鸣钟」——
t_en: As instructed in his will, we collected the parts of his body and reassembled them into the "Haliburton Memorial Chime Clock" that resounds throughout the entire galaxy...

t_ch: 并在星际和平公司的纪度慈善拍卖上拍出了两百亿信用点的高价。
t_en: We then sold it for <unbreak>20</unbreak> billion credits at the IPC's annual charity auction.

t_ch: 我的师父，他真是位伟大的工匠。您觉得呢？
t_en: My master was truly a great craftsman. Don't you think?

t_ch: 换个问题。
t_en: Next question.

t_ch: 暂时没有别的消息。
t_en: I have no more news right now.

t_ch: 再见，也希望您度过一个美好的夜晚。
t_en: Farewell. I hope that you enjoy a wonderful evening, too.

t_ch: 暂时没有别的消息。
t_en: I have no more news right now.

t_ch: 再见，也希望您度过一个美好的夜晚。
t_en: Farewell. I hope that you enjoy a wonderful evening, too.

t_ch: 哈利伯顿看着远方的匹诺康尼歌剧院陷入沉思。
t_en: Haliburton falls into a thoughtful silence as he stares at the distant Penacony theater.

t_ch: 悬浮在高空中的歌剧院…或许那里就是「钟表匠」的藏身之处？
t_en: A theater that floats high in the sky... Perhaps that is where the Watchmaker would be hiding?

t_ch: 除非藏在天上，不然怎么会找不到他呢？
t_en: How could I not find him, unless he was hidden in the skies?

t_ch: 我的心脏…似乎…不太对劲……
t_en: My heart... Something... Doesn't feel right...

t_ch: 等等……这是……什么感觉……
t_en: Wait... What... What is this feeling...

t_ch: 哈利伯顿胸口的嘀嗒声越发缓慢，嘀…嗒…嘀……嗒……
t_en: The ticking sound in Haliburton's chest is growing slower and slower... Tick... Tock...... Tick...... Tock......

t_ch: 是……零件……或者动力源……出……问题……了…………
t_en: One of...... The parts...... Or maybe...... The energy source...... There's...... Something wrong.........
Everything... Is getting...... Slower............ And Slower...............

t_ch: 哈利伯顿的胸口中传来急促而沉重的滴答声。
t_en: A rapid and heavy ticking sound is coming from inside Haliburton's chest.

t_ch: 是什么这么热——哦不，它转得太快了——太快了！！
t_en: Why is it so hot... Oh, no! It's going too fast... It's too fast!!

t_ch: 这颗心脏还不够稳定——超于负荷——它会、它会——
t_en: This heart of mine is still unstable... If it is overloaded, it will... It will...

t_ch: 「嘭！！！」有什么东西从卡死的组件间弹出来，落在你手中。
t_en: *Boom!!* Something flies out from between the jammed parts and lands in your hands.

t_ch: 哈利伯顿的胸口中传来急促而沉重的滴答声。
t_en: A rapid and heavy ticking sound is coming from inside Haliburton's chest.

t_ch: 心脏跳得太快——齿轮会——卡死的——
t_en: My heart is going too fast... The gears are... Going to jam...

t_ch: 咦？我的心脏好像出了点问题……？
t_en: Huh? I think there is something wrong with my heart...?

t_ch: 很奇妙的感觉，像是加多了机油，轴承的转动变得顺滑又灵敏……
t_en: This feels very strange... The bearings are rotating smoothly and flexibly as if I've added too much engine oil...

t_ch: 嘀嗒、嘀嗒！我第一次在钟表上听到如此轻盈的计时声，到底是哪里的变化？
t_en: Tick tock, tick tock! This is the first time I've heard such a light chiming on a clock. What is different here?

t_ch: 因为感受到了「喜悦」。
t_en: It's because you felt "joy."

t_ch: 除了物质原料外，「情感」也能影响表针的跃动吗？
t_en: So, it's not just the raw materials themselves... do even "emotions" influence the rhythm of clocks?

t_ch: 这可真是了不起的大发现！
t_en: This is truly an astounding discovery!

t_ch: 这是梦境，什么都可能发生。
t_en: This is the Dreamscape, where anything can happen.

t_ch: 正因身处梦境之中，所以机械的心脏也能感受到「喜悦」吗？
t_en: My robotic heart is able to feel "joy" because I am inside a dream?

t_ch: 或许变化是和此处的忆质有关——这可真是了不起的大发现！
t_en: Perhaps this change is due to the memoria in this place... This is truly an astounding discovery!

t_ch: 哦，请原谅我的激动——或许是我还未能适应这份名为「喜悦」的情感！
t_en: Ah, please forgive my excitement... Perhaps I am not yet used to this emotion you call "joy"!

t_ch: 这是我的谢礼，请您务必收下！
t_en: This is a gift from me. You must take it!

t_ch: 我第一次在钟表上听到如此轻盈的计时声，像是加多了机油，轴承的转动变得顺滑又灵敏……
t_en: This is the first time I've heard such a light chiming on a clock. The bearings are rotating smoothly and flexibly as if I've added too much engine oil...

t_ch: 原来这就是「喜悦」的感觉！
t_en: So, this is what "joy" feels like!

t_ch: 嘿，朋友，给你讲个好玩的事。昨天我站在柜台后，听到有人说打劫，吓得我汽水都喷出来了，可我找了半天都没看到人影…你知道是为什么吗？
t_en: Hey, pal! Let me tell you something fun! I had a close call with a robbery yesterday. I was standing behind the counter when someone shouted, "This is a stick up!" I was so startled that I even spat out my soda. But guess what? When I turned around, there was nobody there... Can you guess why?

t_ch: …因为那个人能隐身？
t_en: ...Because the robber was invisible?

t_ch: …因为躲在你的背后？
t_en: ...Because the robber was hiding behind you?

t_ch: 因为那是个皮皮西人——哈哈哈哈！
t_en: Because the robber was a Pepeshi! Hahahaha!

t_ch: 从柜台后面走出来，我才注意到这个拿刀的劫匪，他说「你好，打劫！」，我说「呃，你这样也没法威胁我啊」，他说「有道理，我得找个能直接伤害你的位置」。
t_en: I didn't even notice the robber holding a knife until I stepped out from behind the counter. He said, "Good afternoon, give me all your money!" So, I replied, "Uh, you don't really look intimidating, though." He agreed and said, "You're right. I should find a place where I can actually harm you."

t_ch: 然后，他顺着那边的餐桌爬上了柜台，结果刚站稳就晕了过去…你知道是为什么吗？
t_en: He climbed up on the counter using that table over there, but as soon as he stood still... he passed out. Can you guess why?

t_ch: …因为他没吃早饭？
t_en: ...Because he hadn't eaten breakfast?

t_ch: …因为他营养不良？
t_en: ...Because he was starving?

t_ch: 因为那是个恐高的皮皮西人——哈哈哈哈！
t_en: Because that Pepeshi has a fear of heights! Hahahaha!

t_ch: 噢，请相信，我不是对皮皮西人抱有什么偏见。相反，我非常尊敬他们，甚至认为他们才应当是这个世界的主宰，有个著名的理论曾表述过相同的观点…你知道是什么理论吗？
t_en: Oh, please trust me. I don't hold any prejudice against anyone, including the Pepeshi. On the contrary, I have great respect for them, to the point that I think they should rule this world. There's even a famous theory that shares a similar perspective... Do you know what it is?

t_ch: …破窗效应？
t_en: ...The broken windows effect?

t_ch: …杠杆原理？
t_en: ...The principle of leverage?

t_ch: 木桶效应！最短的那块木板决定一切——哈哈哈哈！
t_en: Law of the minimum! The capacity of the barrel is determined by the "shortest" stave. Hahahaha!

t_ch: 你还是做好服务生的工作吧。
t_en: You should focus on your work as a waiter.

t_ch: 你还真是没幽默感。罢了罢了，说吧，想吃点什么？
t_en: Aw, come on, where's your sense of humor? So, what would you like to eat?

t_ch: 你就是个天生的脱口秀演员！
t_en: You've got some natural comedic talent!

t_ch: 是吧是吧！讲皮皮西笑话可比做服务生有意思多了！
t_en: You think so, right? Telling jokes about the Pepeshi is way more fun than waiting tables!

t_ch: …我不感兴趣。
t_en: ...I'm not interested.

t_ch: 记得收听「奇异果」电台的脱口秀演出啊！
t_en: Don't forget to tune in to the Kiwis' stand-up comedy show!

t_ch: 没有不好笑的段子，只有不会讲笑话的演员！
t_en: There is no such thing as bad jokes, only lousy comedians who can't deliver them!

t_ch: 怎么样，我讲脱口秀的水平是不是都足够去匹诺康尼大剧院表演了？
t_en: So, do you think I have what it takes to perform at the Penacony Theater?

t_ch: 对不起，我要向所有的皮皮西人道歉。
t_en: I'm sorry, I should apologize to all the Pepeshi out there.

t_ch: 完了…全完了……
t_en: I'm busted... I'm totally busted...

t_ch: 就前天，我正讲着刚想的笑话呢，回过头一看，餐厅新来的领班居然站在后面盯着我。更倒霉的是，他是个皮皮西人。
t_en: Just the other day, I was thinking about a new joke, and when I turned around, the new manager was standing there, staring at me. And to make matters worse, he's a Pepeshi.

t_ch: 他说：「虽然我打不到你的脸，但你会主动弯腰给我打的，对吧？」
t_en: He said, "While I'm not tall enough to punch you in the face, you will voluntarily bend over and let me punch you, won't you?"

t_ch: …还好，工作是保住了，工资是扣光了；信念是守护了，信用点是守不住了。
t_en: ...Fortunately, I managed to keep my job, but my paycheck took a hit. While my dreams were intact, my pride got bruised.

t_ch: 都怪我这张没遮没拦的嘴！！
t_en: It's all because of my big mouth!

t_ch: 不想成为脱口秀演员的餐厅侍者不是好的家族成员！
t_en: A restaurant waiter who doesn't want to become a stand-up comedian is not a good Family member!

t_ch: 嗯…好吧，我知道那些段子很不专业，但确实好玩。谁叫我疯狂地爱上了脱口秀呢。
t_en: Well... fine. I admit those jokes were not professional, but they were fun. I suppose it's my own fault for falling head over heels for stand-up comedy.

t_ch: 活着总得有个梦想，我就是要成为匹诺康尼最伟大的脱口秀演员！
t_en: Everyone needs a dream to chase, and mine is to become Penacony's greatest stand-up comedian!

t_ch: …你知道「奇异果」嘛，最著名的脱口秀团队，尽管投了十一次简历都失败了，但我还是想要加入他们，这将会是我辉煌演艺生涯的第一步！加油！
t_en: ...Have you heard of the Kiwis, the most renowned stand-up comedy team? I've been trying to join their ranks, and they've turned me down eleven times, but the twelfth time is the charm, right? This will be my first step towards my career!

t_ch: 什么都好，快让我想到几个好玩的段子吧！
t_en: Just let me come up with some new jokes! Anything will do!

t_ch: …我编的段子怎么就不好笑了！不至于十一次笔试都不过吧！
t_en: ...Are my jokes really not funny? Why did they reject me eleven times!?

t_ch: 毫无审美的老东西，根本就不懂现在的流行趋势，笑话就是要够俗，贴合实际！
t_en: Those old-school folks just don't get the latest trends!

t_ch: 我不要在这里做一辈子的服务员啊！
t_en: I won't be working as a waiter here forever!

t_ch: 圆台之上，有人指挥着无人演奏的乐器演奏着。
t_en: Someone is directing the performance of musicianless instruments onstage.

t_ch: 凝神望去，最先看到的却是那双手——
t_en: When you look over there, you see a pair of hands...

t_ch: 那双手保养得当、指节修长，翻飞跃动激起黑白分明的波浪。
t_en: Well-maintained and adorned with slender knuckles, they flutter and leap, creating waves of black and white.

t_ch: 那波浪正因轻抚而欢悦、因敲击而昂扬。于是你看见金色音符盘旋升起，喧嚣又美丽。
t_en: Within those waves, joy resides, carried by each stroke that sends golden notes soaring, resounding with beauty.

t_ch: 那音符引领你继续攀援而上。你终于得见舞台的全貌——
t_en: These notes guide your ascent, leading you to a grand vista of the magnificent stage.

t_ch: 钢琴家嘴角含笑、恣意轻松；被齐声合奏的乐器们环绕、拥簇其中。
t_en: The pianist appears serene and content, enveloped by the harmonious music of the instruments.

t_ch: 于是你继续追寻着黄金的音符。你们一路相伴，相携登上顶峰，俯瞰万里夜空；又跃入汪洋，溅起千丈白浪。
t_en: Following the trail of golden notes, you ascend to the pinnacle, beholding the night sky and plunging into an ocean, creating thousands of white capped waves.

t_ch: 不知不觉之间，一曲终了——
t_en: Before you know it, the music reaches its conclusion...

t_ch: 你蓦然惊醒，而演奏者与被演奏者的美梦还在继续。
t_en: You awaken abruptly, yet the dream of the pianist and his instruments persists.

t_ch: 靠近舞台。
t_en: You draw closer to the stage.

t_ch: 钢琴家已全然忘我，沉沦却又漂浮在音乐与欢乐织就的洪流之中。
t_en: The pianist has surrendered himself, immersed in a torrent of music and ecstasy.

t_ch: 兴致高昂时他仍会不自觉哼上几句，却早已换了活泼欢快的调子。
t_en: He hums a few lines when inspiration strikes, transforming his melody into a lively rhythm.

t_ch: 离开。
t_en: Leave.

t_ch: 你明白，此刻已再没什么能够搅扰他了。
t_en: You realize that nothing can break his immersion now.

t_ch: 你喜欢我们的演出吗——你喜欢他的音乐吗？
t_en: Did you enjoy our performance? Did you like his music?

t_ch: 一位和蔼的老妇人突然向你搭话。你环顾四周，却发现这轻飘飘的耳语，竟来自眼前的钢琴。
t_en: A gentle voice suddenly addresses you. As you look around, you realize that it comes from the piano in front of you.

t_ch: 我很高兴——我的小先生，他现在很幸福。
t_en: I'm glad that my dear master... he's happy now.

t_ch: 我从小看着他长大，我们就像彼此的家人一样。
t_en: I've been accompanying him since he was a child. We are like family.

t_ch: 这么多年了，他走到哪儿，就把我带到哪儿…你看，就连在梦里他都没有抛下我。
t_en: Throughout the years, wherever he goes, he takes me along... Even in his dreams, he doesn't leave me behind.

t_ch: 可惜这么好的孩子，却被自己曾经最要好的「朋友们」给抛下了……
t_en: It's a shame that such a kind boy was abandoned by those whom he considered his "best friends"...

t_ch: 我很高兴他愿意来到这座梦中的城市、愿意和自动乐团的大伙儿成为同伴。
t_en: I'm glad that he's willing to come to this dream city and find companions in the automated orchestra.

t_ch: 你瞧，我们爱他、人们也爱他——我的小先生啊，他将再也不会感到孤独、感到痛苦啦。
t_en: You see, we love him, and everyone loves him... My dear master will never feel lonely or miserable again.

t_ch: 去去去、离远点，别捣乱！
t_en: Shoo! Shoo! Don't disrupt us!

t_ch: 耳边突然响起一声暴躁的低吼，你被吓了个激灵。
t_en: A low, irritable growl emerges by your ears, giving you a scare.

t_ch: 你靠得太近了！离远点，别影响我们演出！
t_en: You're too close! Stay away! Don't interfere with our performance!

t_ch: 见你傻愣愣站着不动，一旁的青年——或者说架子鼓——终于忍不住了，扬起支闲着的鼓槌，在你手臂上不轻不重地敲了一下。
t_en: Seeing you still standing there, the young man on the side, more specifically, the "drum," raises his idle drumstick and gives you a not-so-gentle tap on the arm.

t_ch: 跟你说话呢，给个反应啊！果然人类就没有靠谱的——当然，麦克斯除外。
t_en: Hey, I'm talking to you! Are all humans so numb? Well, except Max, of course.

t_ch: 等等…你…你该不会打算拐走麦克斯吧？想都别想！
t_en: Wait... You... You're not planning to take Max away, are you? Don't even think about it!

t_ch: 我告诉你，我们才是他*最重要*的伙伴，永远都是！
t_en: I'm warning you! We are his *most important* companions, and we will always be!

t_ch: *自动乐团*能满足他的一切需求，所以别再打他的主意了！
t_en: The *automated orchestra* can fulfill his every need! Don't ever try to take him away!

t_ch: 您很关注麦克斯先生…是因为感到好奇吗？
t_en: Are you concerned about Maestro Max... out of curiosity?

t_ch: 一位优雅绅士不紧不慢地开口。
t_en: A sophisticated gentleman spoke nonchalantly.

t_ch: 很抱歉擅自揣测您的想法，不过我听*钢琴夫人*说过，麦克斯先生在「现实中」似乎与*现在*不大一样。
t_en: I apologize for assuming your thoughts, but according to *Mrs. Piano*, it seems that Maestro Max isn't quite the same in reality as he is *now*.

t_ch: 如果您之前见过他，那么我非常理解您的好奇心…因为我对他也抱有同样的*好奇*。
t_en: If you have met him before, then I understand your curiosity very well... as I share the same *curiosity* about him.

t_ch: 您知道吗——或许这个说法算不上得体——在我看来，麦克斯先生和我们简直是「天生伴侣」。
t_en: In my opinion, although it may not be entirely appropriate... Maestro Max and we are simply natural partners.

t_ch: 音乐家们往往过分热衷于在音乐中展现自我，这不是件坏事，但对于我们这些自动乐器而言，多少有些冒犯。
t_en: Musicians tend to be overly expressive in their music, which isn't necessarily a negative trait, but it can be overwhelming for us automated instruments.

t_ch: 而麦克斯先生…他在自我塑造的过程中，始终保持着一种忘我的纯粹，这令我们感到非常*舒适*。
t_en: However, Maestro Max... He maintains a sense of innocent purity throughout his self-expression, which puts us at *ease*.

t_ch: 那些「旧友们」并不善于发觉他的天赋，为此我感到十分遗憾。
t_en: His "old friends" failed to recognize his talent, and I feel genuinely sorry about that.

t_ch: 但不要紧，我们会代替他们，成为麦克斯先生最忠实的伙伴。
t_en: Nonetheless, we will step in and become Maestro Max's most loyal companions.

t_ch: 节拍陡然一转，现场的气氛变得热烈起来。
t_en: The beat intensifies, and the atmosphere grows heated.

t_ch: 不知不觉中，人群开始朝这边聚集；连空气渐渐都开始变得灼热而拥挤。
t_en: The crowd gathers, filling the space. Even the air carries a buoyant spirit.

t_ch: 你又看见那些金色音符——它们此刻更加灵动张扬、愈发色泽艳丽。
t_en: Once again, you witness the golden notes, now more dynamic and vibrant than ever.

t_ch: 每一次震颤都令你的心跳随之鼓噪，你的血脉为之偾张——
t_en: Each tremor sets your heart pounding, your veins electrified.

t_ch: 等什么呢？还不快抛弃一切烦恼，彻底融入这音乐的狂欢！
t_en: What are you waiting for? Cast away your worries and immerse yourself in this musical carnival!

t_ch: 过马路的时候请小心。虽然在梦中受伤并不会导致肉体死亡，但被车撞的疼感可是相当拟真的……
t_en: Be careful when you cross the road. Your body won't die if you get wounded in a dream, but the pain from a car crash still hurts like the real thing...

t_ch: …如果疼感造成的冲击过于强烈，你就会从梦中惊醒。换个角度来看，应该没人想在梦境里目睹一场惨烈的车祸吧？
t_en: ...If the impact of the pain is too intense, you'll be startled awake from your dream. In other words, I'm guessing no one in the Dreamscape really wants to witness a horrific car crash?

t_ch: 不过别担心，家族会尽最大的努力保护每一位宾客。我们有许多维持梦境世界安定的手段。
t_en: But don't worry, The Family protects each and every guest with utmost care. We have many security measures to maintain the Dreamscape's safety.

t_ch: 你是交通志愿者吗？
t_en: Are you a traffic volunteer?

t_ch: 哈哈，我倒是很乐意为贡献自己的一份力量。
t_en: Haha, I'm more than happy to lend a hand.

t_ch: 不过呢，我其实是猎犬家系的护卫组长。守护行人安全是我的职责。
t_en: But, I'm actually the Bloodhound Family's Guard Leader. Protecting travelers is my duty.

t_ch: 作为来宾，希望你能遵守《入梦守则》，不要给他人造成负面的梦境体验。
t_en: As a guest, I hope you will abide by the Dreaming Regulations, and refrain from burdening others with a bad experience in the Dreamscape.

t_ch: …至于存心危害梦境的家伙，我们会严肃地对他做出提醒——如果能从一开始就避免走到这步的话，那真是再好不过了。
t_en: ...As for those offenders who seek to sully the Dreamscape, we will sternly issue them with a warning — it would be for the best if one would avoid this outcome in the first place.

t_ch: 你是被罚来站岗的吗？
t_en: Are you being punished with guard duty?

t_ch: 依我看，守护来宾的安全可不能算一种惩罚。
t_en: From where I stand, protecting the safety of guests is no punishment at all.

t_ch: 这确实是十分基础的工作，但基础不代表没有价值。家族的事业离不开为之付出的奉献者，唯有团结协作才能铸就伟大。
t_en: This may be a very basic job, but that doesn't mean it is meaningless. The Family's businesses rely plenty on patrons who contribute to them. Only in unity can greatness be achieved.

t_ch: 你不是家族成员，不了解这个道理也很正常。我原谅你的失礼。
t_en: You're not a Family member, so it's only natural you don't understand our ways. I forgive your insolence.

t_ch: 有些问题想要问你…
t_en: I have some questions for you...

t_ch: 那你算问对人了——服务来宾是每一名家族成员的责任。我会尽力回答你的。
t_en: You've asked the right person then. Servicing guests is one of the duties of every Family member. I will answer as best as I can.

t_ch: 家族是什么？
t_en: What is The Family?

t_ch: 家族是梦境的缔造者和管理者，在匹诺康尼有着举足轻重的地位。
t_en: The Family is both the creator and administrator of the Dreamscape, and they are of decisive importance in Penacony.

t_ch: 除了我们猎犬家系外，还有橡木、苜蓿草、隐夜鸫和鸢尾花四大家系。各个家系分别负责不同的工作，彼此亲密无间。
t_en: Apart from us Bloodhound Family, there are also four other major lineages of Oak, Alfalfa, Nightingale, and Iris. Each family is charged with different jobs and is very tight-knit.

t_ch: 真的亲密无间？
t_en: Are they really?

t_ch: 当然，这可没有夸大的成分。家族之所以能把梦境打理得井井有条，就是因为各家系能精诚团结，全无冲突与分歧。
t_en: Of course. That is not hyperbole. The only reason that The Family was able to make sure the Dreamscape runs in such an orderly manner is due to the various families' earnest unity. We are all without conflict or disagreement.

t_ch: 在偌大的宇宙中，个人的力量实在太过渺小。唯有相互帮扶，万众一心，凡人才可能做出非凡的壮举。
t_en: In the vast universe, the strength of an individual is insignificant. Only through mutual help and binding our hearts as one, can humans perform superhuman feats.

t_ch: 这也是{RUBY_B#「同谐」}神主{RUBY_E#}对我们的教导，任何人都应铭记在心。
t_en: This is also the {RUBY_B#Harmony}Great One's{RUBY_E#} teachings unto us. Everyone would do well to remember them by heart.

t_ch: 猎犬家系是做什么的？
t_en: What does the Bloodhound Family do?

t_ch: 我们猎犬在五大家系中主管安保与维和。不只是警卫，消防员、侦探和其他一些职位也由我们的成员负责。
t_en: Among the five lineages, we the Bloodhounds manage security and maintain order. It's not just the police — our members are also in charge of the firemen, detectives, and other vocations.

t_ch: 想象一下猎犬的品质吧——机灵，善战，而且忠诚。这也是每名猎犬成员的美德。我们是匹诺康尼美梦的后盾，宾客安全的保障。
t_en: Think about the characteristics of a hound: Clever, battle-hardy, loyal. These are also the virtues of each Bloodhound member. We are the shield of Penacony's beautiful dream, safekeeping the well-being of our guests.

t_ch: 当然，如果有人不把《入梦守则》放在眼里，那么他很快就会意识到猎犬是多么可怕且难缠的对手。
t_en: Of course, if someone flouts the Dreaming Regulations, he will very quickly learn how frightening and persistent the Bloodhounds are.

t_ch: 暂时没有问题了。
t_en: I have no questions right now.

t_ch: 注意安全。如果遇到危险，猎犬随时为你效劳。
t_en: Watch yourself. If you run into danger, the Bloodhounds are at your service.

t_ch: 哦对了，难得有人主动跟我搭话，这个送给你吧。这是一位橡木家系的朋友送给我的…但我不喜欢甜食。
t_en: Oh right, since it's rare that someone would bother to talk to me, I'll give this to you. A friend from the Oak Family gave it to me... but I don't have a sweet tooth.

t_ch: 有些问题想要问你…
t_en: I have some questions for you...

t_ch: 那你算问对人了——服务来宾是每一名家族成员的责任。我会尽力回答你的。
t_en: You've asked the right person then. Servicing guests is one of the duties of every Family member. I will answer as best as I can.

t_en: What is The Family?

t_ch: 家族是梦境的缔造者和管理者，在匹诺康尼有着举足轻重的地位。
t_en: The Family is both the creator and administrator of the Dreamscape, and they are of decisive importance in Penacony.

t_en: Are they really?

t_ch: 当然，这可没有夸大的成分。家族之所以能把梦境打理得井井有条，就是因为各家系能精诚团结，全无冲突与分歧。
t_en: Of course. That is not hyperbole. The only reason that The Family was able to make sure the Dreamscape runs in such an orderly manner is due to the various families' earnest unity. We are all without conflict or disagreement.

t_en: In the vast universe, the strength of an individual is insignificant. Only through mutual help and binding our hearts as one, can humans perform superhuman feats.

t_en: This is also the {RUBY_B#Harmony}Great One's{RUBY_E#} teachings unto us. Everyone would do well to remember them by heart.

t_en: What does the Bloodhound Family do?

t_ch: 我们猎犬在五大家系中主管安保与维和。不只是警卫，消防员、侦探和其他一些职位也由我们的成员负责。
t_en: Among the five lineages, we the Bloodhounds manage security and maintain order. It's not just the police — our members are also in charge of the firemen, detectives, and other vocations.

t_ch: 当然，如果有人不把《入梦守则》放在眼里，那么他很快就会意识到猎犬是多么可怕且难缠的对手。
t_en: Of course, if someone flouts the Dreaming Regulations, he will very quickly learn how frightening and persistent the Bloodhounds are.

t_ch: 暂时没有问题了。
t_en: I have no questions right now.

t_ch: 注意安全。如果遇到危险，猎犬随时为你效劳。
t_en: Watch yourself. If you run into danger, the Bloodhounds are at your service.

t_ch: 没事了。
t_en: That's all.

t_ch: 注意安全。如果遇到危险，猎犬随时为你效劳。
t_en: Watch yourself. If you run into danger, the Bloodhounds are at your service.

t_ch: 梦中有哪些值得我关注的人？
t_en: Is anyone worth my attention in the dream?

t_ch: 哦，这个问题真是正中下怀。梦里再找不到比我们猎犬更专业的探子了。
t_en: Oh, this is a good question. There's no one more in the know than us Bloodhounds.

t_ch: 实际上，梦境里确实有一批…比较特殊的人，是猎犬的重点观察对象。
t_en: In truth, there are a bunch of quite... special people in the Dreamscape. They are also our observation targets.

t_ch: 如果你和类似的家伙打过交道，应该明白我指的是什么。
t_en: If you've come across such people, you probably know what I'm talking about.

t_ch: 不过，最近我的艾迪恩代币不够了，所以…咳咳，你懂得。
t_en: But, I've been suffering a shortage of Aideen Tokens recently, so... *ahem* you know what to do.

t_ch: （给他一枚艾迪恩代币）
t_en: (Give him 1 Aideen Token)

t_ch: 那么，你想要关注什么样的对象呢？
t_en: Now then, who do you want to know about?

t_ch: 生不了气的。
t_en: The one who can't get angry.

t_ch: 开心不起来的。
t_en: The one who can't be happy.

t_ch: 哭不出来的。
t_en: The one who can't cry.

t_ch: 冷静不下来的。
t_en: The one who can't calm down.

t_ch: 不用担心意外，家族会保护每一位来宾。
t_en: Don't worry about accidents — The Family will protect each and every guest.

t_ch: 一愣过后，伍尔西又重新恢复了那副冷峻的面孔——不难看出，夸张的情绪外露让他倍感尴尬。
t_en: In an instant, Woolsey returns to his aloof expression. It's not hard to see that the exaggerated outburst has made him feel extremely awkward.

t_ch: 家主真该让那些人好好认识下猎犬！
t_en: The head should really have those guys get acquainted with the Bloodhounds!

t_ch: 啊！商店街那些不长眼的逐梦客，真是一想就来气。
t_en: Bah! I get irate just at the thought of those short-sighted dream-chasing fools on the shopping street.

t_ch: 有些猎犬成员长得是有点凶，但一见我们就跑是不是有点太夸张了——我们又不吃小孩！
t_en: Some Bloodhound members may look pretty fierce, but people don't have to flee at the sight of us... It's not like we eat children!

t_ch: 要是没有我们保护匹诺康尼的梦境，这地界早就乱套了，哪儿轮得到他们夜夜笙歌？！
t_en: If we weren't around to protect Penacony's Dreamscape, this realm would plunge into chaos. Where would they have the idyllic moods to sing the night away!?

t_ch: 这帮家伙不感恩就罢了，还总要摆出一副嘴脸，好像我们上辈子欠了他们钱一样。
t_en: I'd tolerate these buffoons if they were ingrates, but those disapproving faces they make... It's like we owed them money in a past life.

t_ch: 呵，吃里扒外的东西！我绝对不会给他们送钱…给你还差不多。
t_en: Hah, those two-faced fools! I'll never give them any money... I'd much rather give them to you.

t_ch: 啊！商店街那些不长眼的逐梦客，真是一想就来气！
t_en: Bah! I get irate just at the thought of those short-sighted dream-chasing fools on the shopping street!

t_ch: 有些猎犬成员长得是有点凶，但一见我们就跑是不是有点太夸张了——我们又不吃小孩！
t_en: Some Bloodhound members may look pretty fierce, but people don't have to flee at the sight of us... It's not like we eat children!

t_ch: 要是没有我们保护匹诺康尼的梦境，这地界早就乱套了，哪儿轮得到他们夜夜笙歌？！
t_en: If we weren't around to protect Penacony's Dreamscape, this realm would plunge into chaos. Where would they have the idyllic moods to sing the night away!?

t_ch: 这帮家伙不感恩就罢了，还总要摆出一副嘴脸，好像我们上辈子欠了他们钱一样……
t_en: I'd tolerate these buffoons if they were ingrates, but those disapproving faces they make... It's like we owed them money in a past life...

t_ch: 唉，家族的未来可怎么办啊……
t_en: *sigh* What can we do about The Family's future...

t_ch: 现在的家族成员，真是一代不如一代……
t_en: The caliber of The Family's members is really going down the drain with each generation...

t_ch: 我弟弟今年就要举办成人礼了。我问他最喜欢什么活动，他居然说是刺绣…真是枉费我一番苦心，把全套宇宙拳王争霸赛的珍贵录像都送给他了。
t_en: My younger brother will have his coming-of-age rites this year. I asked him what his favorite activity was, and he actually said embroidery... What a complete waste of the trouble I went through — I even gave him the entire Cosmic Fistfighter Championship collection that was worth a fortune.

t_ch: 他那幅孱弱的身板，哪还像个猎犬的模样？要是被家族里的长辈看到了，不笑掉大牙才怪。
t_en: Just look at that puny body of his — how could he resemble anything close to a Bloodhound? If The Family old-timers were to see him, they'd laugh themselves to their graves.

t_ch: 而且我只是随便批评了两句，他就唉声叹气，哭哭啼啼……
t_en: And all I did was reprimand him lightly, and then he just sighed and started bawling...

t_ch: 唉，真愁人。明明当哥哥的也是为了他好，怎么就不懂呢……
t_en: *sigh* So vexing. I'm just trying to be a good older brother to him, why can't he understand...

t_ch: 这样一来，不就显得为这事儿愁眉苦脸的我也像个笑话了吗？唉……
t_en: And now I look like a joke for being worried in the first place? *sigh*

t_ch: 哈哈哈！没有猎犬，这个世界早完蛋了！
t_en: Hahaha! This world would've been a goner without the Bloodhounds!

t_ch: 哦…哦哦哦！不知道为什么，我突然感觉心情变顺畅了——就像刚参与了一场群架，而我是最后一个站着的人！哈哈哈！
t_en: Oh... Oh oh oh! I don't know why, but I suddenly feel much better — like I just joined a bar brawl, and I'm the last one standing! Hahaha!

t_ch: 不仅如此！一想到我是家族的一份子，我的自豪感就源源不断地向外喷涌，如同这城里随处可见的喷钞机！
t_en: Not just that! Just the thought of me being one of The Family makes my heart burst with overflowing pride, like one of those money guns that are everywhere in the city!

t_ch: 哦，我们是猎犬——阴谋的揭露者，匹诺康尼的守护神！不管梦境还是现实，一切危机在我们面前都将烟消云散！
t_en: Oh, we're the Bloodhounds — the expositors of conspiracies, protectors of Penacony! Be it in dreams or reality, all dangers dissipate like smoke before us!

t_ch: 来，跟我一起念——没有猎犬，世界就完蛋了！
t_en: Come, chant with me: The world would be a goner without the Bloodhounds!

t_ch: 猎犬也不能饿着肚子干活——艾迪恩代币就是我的精神食粮。抱歉啦，客人。
t_en: Bloodhounds can't work on an empty stomach. Aideen Tokens are my spiritual food. Sorry about that, honored guest.

t_ch: 已经没有这样的人了。不知为何，这些家伙最近都变得有些…真性情。
t_en: There's no one like that anymore. For some reason, these fellows have all become somewhat... true to their nature recently.

t_ch: 不过我倒不觉得这是件坏事——即使在梦里，大家也拥有保持愤怒的自由。
t_en: But I don't think it's a bad thing. Even in dreams, everyone still has the freedom to remain angry.

t_ch: 已经没有这样的人了。即使最愁眉苦脸的家伙，一想到梦境的繁华与美好，也会不由自主地露出笑颜。
t_en: There's no one like that anymore. Even the most worrisome person would unwittingly smile at the thought of the dream's prosperity and beauty.

t_ch: 匹诺康尼现在是真正的「美梦」了。
t_en: Penacony is now a truly beautiful dream.

t_ch: 已经没有这样的人了。大家都是想哭就哭，想笑就笑。
t_en: There's no one like that anymore. Everyone cries when they wish, and laughs when they want.

t_ch: 虽然不知道怎么回事，但我觉得这样也挺好。有句话怎么说来着：「若哭泣不自由，则欢笑无意义」。
t_en: I don't know what's going on, but I think it's a good thing. How does that saying go again? "If you're not free to cry, then there's no point in laughing."

t_ch: 已经没有这样的人了。现在大家都能控制好自己的情绪，而不是一天到头歇斯底里。
t_en: There's no one like that anymore. Everyone can manage their emotions very well now and isn't in a constant state of hysteria.

t_ch: 必须承认，自从某些人消停之后，我耳边确实清净了许多。
t_en: I must admit that it's been much more peaceful once some people stopped their antics.

t_ch: 嗯…据我所知，没有。
t_en: Hmm... According to what I know, no.

t_ch: 说来奇怪，最近许多梦境中人都变得…不一样了。背后可能有人在做手脚。
t_en: It's strange, but recently, many people in the Dreamscape have become... different. There might be someone causing this behind the scenes.

t_ch: 我们猎犬会持续关注此事，以免影响其他来宾。客人你就放心好了。
t_en: We Bloodhounds will continue to keep an eye on this matter and prevent it from affecting the other guests. Please do not be concerned.

t_ch: 嗯？之前不是刚给你指明过吗，又要找新的人？
t_en: Hmm? Didn't I just tell you that earlier? Looking for someone else?

t_ch: 偶尔也稍微安分一下吧，至少先跟我介绍的那个人打打交道——换朋友不要那么频繁嘛。
t_en: Have some patience and give the person I introduced a chance, okay? Friends aren't something you can just swap out all the time.

t_ch: 这片梦境区域还没有完工，暂时不向游客开放。
t_en: This dreamscape area is still under construction and off-limits to visitors for now.

t_ch: 但既然你能出现在这里，想必是得到了上头的批准，我也就不多问了。
t_en: But since you're here, you must have gotten the thumbs-up from the higher-ups, so I won't ask anything.

t_ch: 总之，有困难，找猎犬。
t_en: Anyway, come to us Bloodhounds if you're in trouble.

t_ch: 这里虽不如大街上繁华，但视野极好，空气也要新鲜不少，还有免费的相声听……
t_en: This place may not be as bustling as the main street, but it's got a good view, and the air is fresher too. And there are free crosstalk performances to listen to...

t_ch: 您好啊，这么巧在此处遇见，要不要一起看会儿风景聊聊天？
t_en: Hello, what a coincidence to see you here. How about a chat while we take in the sight?

t_ch: 这不是工地吗？
t_en: Isn't this a construction site?

t_ch: 怎么还有相声？
t_en: There are crosstalk performances too?

t_ch: 嗨呀，我说的就是那些筑梦师啊，这帮人可好玩了——
t_en: No, I'm talking about those Dreamweavers. They're a fun lot...

t_ch: 你怨我需求乱改，我恨你进度欺诈；理念不合要吵，设计分歧就骂；私下里蜚短流长，当着面撸袖子干架……
t_en: You complain about my constantly changing requirements, I resent your deception in progress. We argue when our ideas don't align, and insults fly when our designs differ. Privately, grievances linger, and face-to-face, sleeves are rolled up for a confrontation.

t_ch: 天天你来我往，就跟说相声似的。这帮「艺术工作者」可真艺术啊…哈哈！
t_en: It's just like performing crosstalk, these "artists" sure are artsy... Heh!

t_ch: 唉，说到这个，好久没回罗浮，也不知道阿来阿往那俩小子最近又有什么新鲜段子……
t_en: *sigh* Speaking of which, it's been a long time since I've gone back to the Luofu. I wonder if Back'n and Forth have come up with any fresh new routines...

t_ch: 我前两天才听过。
t_en: I listened to them just a couple of days ago.

t_ch: 想听西衍先生说书了。
t_en: I want to listen to Mr. Xiyan's storytelling now.

t_ch: 哎——没想到！竟然还是他乡遇故知啊！怪不得今儿日历上说是宜出行，敢情在这儿等着我呢！
t_en: Ooh, how unexpected! A friend from a distant land! No wonder the calendar said that it was good to travel today — an encounter was waiting for me right here!

t_ch: 我向来工作之余就喜欢来这里放松放松心情，小友之后若是得了空，记得多来找我多聊聊啊！
t_en: I like to relax here after work. If you ever find the time, don't forget to stop by and have a chat!

t_ch: 来聊两句吗？
t_en: Can we chat a little?

t_ch: 那敢情好啊，我正愁在这边找不到人聊天呢！
t_en: That would be fantastic. I've been hankering for a chat around here!

t_ch: 你来匹诺康尼做什么？
t_en: What are you doing in Penacony?

t_ch: 还能做什么，做生意呗。我是天舶司下翔水商会驻匹诺康尼的负责人，来这儿是打算开拓一下贸易市场。
t_en: What else can I do? Business, of course. I'm the head of the Aquaglider Guild in Penacony under the Sky-Faring Commission, here to explore the trade market.

t_ch: 目前来说，我的主要业务是向仙舟引进苏乐达。
t_en: Currently, my main business is to import SoulGlad to the Xianzhou.

t_ch: 不过说实在的…我还真不怎么喜欢那种甜了吧唧的气泡糖浆，那玩意也就是广告营销做得好。
t_en: But honestly... I don't really like that saccharine, carbonated syrup. It's just advertised well.

t_ch: 要真论好喝，还得是咱仙舟的仙人快乐茶！
t_en: If you want a REALLY good beverage, it's gotta be Xianzhou's Immortal's Delight!

t_ch: 你的尾巴…
t_en: Your tail...

t_ch: 呃…嗐——这不是好不容易来一次梦里嘛，自然要和平日里做出些区别。
t_en: Err... Well, it's a rare treat to be in a dream — gotta do something that's a little different from your humdrum life.

t_ch: 你别说，现在没了尾巴，感觉还挺神奇的，哈哈！
t_en: Well, it feels wondrously strange without a tail, haha!

t_ch: 我先走啦。
t_en: I'll be heading off now.

t_ch: 好嘞，那您自便，我再呆一会儿。
t_en: Sure thing, you do you. I'll stay a while longer.

t_ch: 我先走啦。
t_en: I'll be heading off now.

t_ch: 慢走，咱们回见！
t_en: Have a good day, we'll meet again soon!

t_ch: 你说这么大个匹诺康尼，就真连个贸易蓝海都没有？
t_en: Is there truly no room for new trade products in such a vast market like Penacony's?

t_ch: 这前前后后我也调研了好几个月了，愣是没找到什么还有开发潜力的贸易口子。
t_en: I've already surveyed the trends for months — I just can't seem to find any potential breakthrough that's worth developing.

t_ch: 生意难做啊…再这么下去，我怕不是真的只能去跟人推销「豆汁儿口味苏乐达」了……
t_en: It's tough doing business here. If things keep up like this, I might end up hawking mung-bean-flavored SoulGlad to others...

t_ch: 别说，我还真的带了苏打豆汁儿过来，你也来一瓶？
t_en: How fortuitous, I have some mung bean soda with me. You want a bottle?

t_ch: 这前前后后我也调研了好几个月了，愣是没找到什么还有开发潜力的贸易口子。
t_en: I've already surveyed the trends for months — I just can't seem to find any potential breakthrough that's worth developing.

t_ch: 生意难做啊…再这么下去，我怕不是真的只能去跟人推销「豆汁儿口味苏乐达」了……
t_en: It's tough doing business here. If things keep up like this, I might end up hawking mung-bean-flavored SoulGlad to others...

t_ch: 我也想要一条尾巴啊…谁不喜欢毛茸茸软乎乎的大尾巴啊……
t_en: I want a tail too... Who doesn't like a poofy fluffy tail...

t_ch: 你们化外民总羡慕长生种，却殊不知越是长生种，越是畏惧天缺。
t_en: You outworlders envy long-life species — what you don't know is the longer we live, the more we dread our genetic disadvantages.

t_ch: 像我这种只是没了尾巴的，尚不影响日常生活，却也百年来都未曾在他人面前抬得起头……
t_en: For those who don't have tails like me, it doesn't affect our daily lives, yet we've never been able to raise our heads high and proud for the last century...

t_ch: 能补天缺倒也不是没有，比如符太卜、比如十王司那个小判官…但这些人要么就是有大毅力、要么就是有大机遇，而我——
t_en: Though it's not like there aren't any who compensate for their disadvantages, like Diviner Fu or that wee judge from the Ten-Lords Commission... But these people either succeed through sheer will, or serendipitous opportunity, yet for me...

t_ch: 我只是个普通人啊。
t_en: I'm but an ordinary person.

t_ch: 老袁突然侧过身，一手捂住腰后，满脸警觉与不耐。
t_en: Yuan suddenly turns sideways, one hand covering the back of her waist, her face awash with alarm and anxiety.

t_ch: 烦不烦、烦不烦、烦不烦！简直没完没了——
t_en: Annoying, annoying, so annoying! Will it never cease!

t_ch: 不就是没尾巴吗！我没有尾巴，难道你们就有咯？
t_en: It's just a tail! So what? Do any of you have tails?

t_ch: 自己都没有，还天天问我！就说烦不烦人、讨不讨厌！
t_en: They don't even have one, yet incessantly ask me about it! So annoying! So sickening!

t_ch: 老袁眺望着远处的天空与高楼，脸上流露出些许惬意的神色。
t_en: Yuan gazes at the sky and towering buildings in the distance, a pleasant expression on her face.

t_ch: 起初我加入翔水商会，其实是为了寻找让自己长出尾巴的机缘。
t_en: When I joined the Aquaglider Guild, I wanted to find an opportunity to grow a tail.

t_ch: 但随着这些年东奔西走，我渐渐明白，自己的人生未必非得绕着那条尾巴转悠。
t_en: But as I traveled across the lands over the years, I gradually understood that my lot in life didn't have to revolve around this tail.

t_ch: 于是我更加留心沿途的风景、各地的人情…不得不说，这或许才是人生真正的意义所在。
t_en: So I started paying more attention to the scenery along the way and the local customs... I have to say, this may really be the true meaning of life.

t_ch: 哎呀，又一个新面孔…你好啊年轻人，愿意和我这个老太婆聊聊吗？
t_en: Ah, a new face... Hello, young one. Mind having a chat with an old lady like me?

t_ch: 好啊，反正现在也没事。
t_en: Sure, I've got nothing to do now anyway.

t_ch: 真是个干脆的年轻人，我喜欢。
t_en: A straightforward one, I like you.

t_ch: 你不是在和他们聊天吗？
t_en: Aren't you chatting with them?

t_ch: 他们…不用在意这些人，他们有自己的事要忙，对吧？
t_en: They... no need to mind these people. They're busy with their own stuff, aren't they?

t_ch: 靠近些，年轻人。你有什么有趣的经历吗，来给老婆子我讲讲吧？
t_en: Come closer, young one. Do you have any interesting experiences to share with an old lady?

t_ch: 本着尊老爱幼的传统美德，你耐着性子和老妇人聊了起来。
t_en: In line with the tradition of respecting your elders, you summon your patience and chat with the old lady.

t_ch: 你给她讲列车与同伴、讲迄今为止的旅途。老妇人专注地听着，渐渐露出餍足的神色。
t_en: You talk to her about your Express companions, about the journey thus far. The old lady listens intently, her expression gradually growing sated.

t_ch: 真好…真好啊！多么自由、多么精彩……
t_en: Very good... Excellent! So free, so exciting...

t_ch: 老妇人眯起眼，喃喃赞叹。你看着她的表情，觉得自己又做了件好事。
t_en: She closes her eyes and murmurs compliments. Seeing her expression, you feel like you've done another good deed.

t_ch: …但她为什么一直盯着你？这让你有点犯怵。她好像在等着你开口，但你完全不知道现在还能说些什么。
t_en: ...But why does she keep staring at you? You're a little apprehensive — she appears to be waiting for you to continue talking, but you don't know what else to say.

t_ch: …那我就先走了？
t_en: ...I should probably leave now?

t_ch: 祝您身体健康…
t_en: I wish you good health...

t_ch: 嗯…？等等，年轻人，别走呀！你还没向我提出*报酬*呢！
t_en: Hmm...? Hold on, young one, don't leave just yet! You haven't asked for a *reward* from me!

t_ch: 什么报酬？
t_en: What reward?

t_ch: …当然是陪我聊天的报酬呀！
t_en: ...For the chat, of course!

t_ch: 你这{F#小姑娘}{M#小伙子}还挺实诚，真不错。现在的年轻人啊，个个能说会道，还知道体贴人，陪老婆子我聊上一整天都不嫌烦。
t_en: You're an honest {F#girl}{M#boy}. Young people nowadays are really good with people, and know how to treat them well. They don't feel bothered even after chatting with an old lady like me for an entire day.

t_ch: 我还挺喜欢你的…要不你就别走了？想要什么只管开口，只要是用钱能满足的需求，我都可以帮你实现。
t_en: I like you quite a bit... How about you stay here? All you have to do is ask — as long as it can be settled with money, I can make all your wishes come true.

t_ch: 喏，这些你先收着吧，就当是我的一点心意。
t_en: Here, keep these. Take it as a token of my appreciation.

t_ch: 还要聊聊吗？
t_en: Still want to chat?

t_ch: 当然当然，我就喜欢跟年轻人聊天……
t_en: Absolutely, I love chatting with young people...

t_ch: 你是什么人？
t_en: Who are you?

t_ch: 哈哈，看来你是真的一点儿都没听说过我啊……
t_en: Haha, looks like you've really never heard of me...

t_ch: 我出身商贾之家…我们老钱家啊，世世代代都在和钱打交道。
t_en: I come from a merchant family... We've been dealing with money for generations.

t_ch: 尚未成年时我就被钦点为下任家主，也算是为咱的家族任劳任怨了一辈子。
t_en: I was named the next head before I came of age, but at least I slogged my whole life for my Family.

t_ch: 这不，到了晚年，好容易得了清闲，我赶紧出来溜达溜达。
t_en: To have the time now for idle times in my golden years, you bet I'm going to come out for a stroll.

t_ch: 那些年轻人…
t_en: These young people...

t_ch: 老婆子我啊，忙了半辈子、累了半辈子，也是时候享享清福了。
t_en: An old granny like me, after hustling for half my life, it's time for me to enjoy some peace and quiet.

t_ch: 我来这匹诺康尼是为了养老，但那些新鲜刺激的已经不适合我了，所以平日里没事就喜欢和年轻人聊聊天……
t_en: I came to Penacony as a retirement plan, but those intense experiences don't suit me at all, which is why I prefer talking to young people in my spare time...

t_ch: 你看，我有故事听、他们有钱拿，这不是双赢吗？
t_en: Look, I get to listen to stories, and they get to earn some money, so that's a win-win all around, no?

t_ch: 你脸色看起来不太好？
t_en: You look a bit under the weather?

t_ch: 哈…说来好笑，我被人惦记了一辈子，没想到竟在这梦里翻了车。
t_en: Ha... It's a funny story — I was targeted my entire life, but I never expected to get up-ended in this dream.

t_ch: 谁能想到这「美梦之城」的大街上，还能突然窜出个皮皮西来，把我这老婆子的脖子给勒了呢？
t_en: Who would've expected that a Pepeshi would suddenly appear on the streets of this city of dreams and strangle an old lady like me?

t_ch: 虽然不过是噩梦一场，但从梦里惊醒的那种感觉可真是不好受哇…希望别再来一次了，我这把年纪可经不起这吓……
t_en: It may have been just a nightmare, but to be shocked awake from my dream didn't feel good at all... I hope it doesn't happen again, I don't think I can take much more at this age...

t_ch: 不管怎么样，都得让家族的人给我个交代……
t_en: In any case, The Family has to take responsibility for this...

t_ch: 先聊到这吧。
t_en: That's enough chit-chat for now.

t_ch: 好、好，有空记得再过来啊。
t_en: Sure, sure. Come by again when you're free.

t_ch: 那回见。
t_en: I'll see you again.

t_ch: 回见，年轻人。我还等着你给我讲更多的故事呢。
t_en: Another time, young one. I'm sure you have more stories to tell.

t_ch: 这位老太太似乎在「让你接受她的资助」这件事上格外执着。
t_en: This old lady appears to be highly insistent on the matter of you accepting her sponsorship.

t_ch: …听说购物中心的珠宝店新出了一套钻石首饰，你想要吗？
t_en: ...I hear the jeweler at the mall has recently launched a set of diamond jewelry. Do you want it?

t_ch: 开拓者一定喜欢车子吧，我送一辆最新款斯坦威游隼给你怎么样？
t_en: Trailblazers must love cars. How about I give you the latest Steinway Falcon speedster?

t_ch: 或者还有什么你看得上眼的，只管说就是。别看我这一套行头朴素，但在正经事上我可从来不吝啬。
t_en: Or there might be something else you fancy, just let me know. I may look like a commoner, but I'm never stingy where it matters.

t_ch: 这个「正经事」嘛，过去对我而言是做买卖谈生意、为家族牟利…而现在，我只想让自己开心。
t_en: As to how I got my money, I used to deal in trade in my younger days, turning profits for The Family... But now, I just want to be happy.

t_ch: 来，这些你拿去…这地方到处是年轻人玩的游戏，我玩不明白，不如你拿着吧。
t_en: Here, take these... The games around here are all for young people. I simply don't get it, so you might as well take them.

t_ch: …听说购物中心的珠宝店新出了一套钻石首饰，你想要吗？
t_en: ...I hear the jeweler at the mall has recently launched a set of diamond jewelry. Do you want it?

t_ch: 开拓者一定喜欢车子吧，我送一辆最新款斯坦威游隼给你怎么样？
t_en: Trailblazers must love cars. How about I give you the latest Steinway Falcon speedster?

t_ch: 或者还有什么你看得上眼的，只管说就是。别看我这一套行头朴素，但在正经事上我可从来不吝啬。
t_en: Or there might be something else you fancy, just let me know. I may look like a commoner, but I'm never stingy where it matters.

t_ch: 这个「正经事」嘛，过去对我而言是做买卖谈生意、为家族牟利…而现在，我只想让自己开心。
t_en: As to how I got my money, I used to deal in trade in my younger days, turning profits for The Family... But now, I just want to be happy.

t_ch: 哈哈，你说可笑不可笑……
t_en: Haha, I'm sure you find it funny...

t_ch: …那帮人整天惦记着从我这儿打听出个「生意经」来，可实际上哪有那玩意啊。
t_en: ...All that bunch of people are obsessed about is getting some business insights out of me. It's all nothing but nonsense if you ask me.

t_ch: 我不过是尽量把事想得周全、做得谨慎，但哪个正经做生意的不是这样呢？
t_en: I just did things as best I could, as cautious as I tried. Any business owner would do the same thing, no?

t_ch: 就算真有什么值得说道的…我始终没被财势迷了眼，这应该能算得上一种好品质？
t_en: Even if I did have something tangible to say... I didn't get blinded by the allure of untold wealth, so I guess it was ethical on my part?

t_ch: 他们只看到我向来生活得清苦朴素，为了省钱凡事亲力亲为，就偷偷称我「吝啬鬼」、「守财奴」…让人难过！
t_en: All they saw was the frugal life I led, doing everything myself in order to save money, and they called me a miser and a scrooge behind my back... I was so sad!

t_ch: 人啊，一旦开始习惯了享受，才真变成了「金钱的奴隶」呢。
t_en: Once people get accustomed to luxury, they become slaves to wealth.

t_ch: 这点道理都不懂，还想把生意做大做好？我看他们是真的把梦当成现实了吧……
t_en: They think they can run a successful business when they don't even know this simple fact? I think they truly believe their dreams have become reality...

t_ch: 唉，你啊，怎么就是不愿意接受我的长期「资助」呢……
t_en: Hey, why are you not willing to accept my long-term sponsorship?

t_ch: 看看我身边那些年轻人…大家各取所需，多好？
t_en: Just take a look at the young people around me... Everyone takes what they need, isn't that good?

t_ch: 唉…我当然知道钱换不来「真心」，但正因如此我才更放心。
t_en: *sigh*... Of course I know money can't buy sincerity, but that's why I feel even more at ease.

t_ch: 我自小颇受家族器重，家中无论平辈长辈，待我总是恭恭敬敬的。
t_en: Since my youth, I was treated with respect by my family, from my peers to my elders.

t_ch: 等到长大了…生意场上哪有什么两肋插刀的朋友？能不被人家惦记上、背后插你两刀已经实属难得。
t_en: When I grew up... Where would you even find true friends who'd do anything for you in the business world? You'd be glad if you went unnoticed, and that all you got was a knife in the back.

t_ch: 我从未识得过「真心」…所以说啊，与其妄想着有人真心相待，还不如花钱买快乐实在。
t_en: I have never experienced sincerity... which is why, instead of fantasizing about someone treating you sincerely, it'd be better to buy happiness and authenticity with money.

t_ch: 不过，或许就是因为你目前还别无所图，我才与你说了这么多吧……
t_en: Then again, perhaps it's because you've shown no ill intent so far, that I've spoken so much to you...

t_ch: 金钱当然是万能的……
t_en: Money is omnipotent of course...

t_ch: 财富对我这种生于商贾之家的人而言，比起享乐的源泉，更像是一种责任。
t_en: To one born into a merchant family like me, wealth resembles more of a responsibility than a source of pleasure.

t_ch: 为了不让偌大家业毁于手中，这么多年来，我都在努力与「奢侈」保持距离。
t_en: To prevent the business from going under, I've been working hard to distance myself from luxury all these years.

t_ch: 我不住豪宅、不买豪车，吃穿用度能省就省，家务维修亲力亲为…说实话，我一直过得很辛苦。
t_en: I don't live in a spacious mansion, I don't buy luxury cars, I scrimp and save on what I eat and wear, and I do my own chores... Honestly, I've been living a harsh life.

t_ch: 但现在，我终于能放下责任，用「普通人」的方式来对待钱了。
t_en: But now, I can finally lay down my duty, and treat money in the way that an ordinary person can.

t_ch: 啊…这挥金如土的感觉，可真是该死的甜美啊！
t_en: Ah... it's a blasted good feeling to spend money as if it meant nothing!

t_ch: 拿去吧，再多拿走点！在年轻人身上花的钱越多，我就越有活着的感觉……
t_en: Take it, take more of it, in fact! The more I spend on young people, the more alive I feel...

t_ch: 财富对我这种生于商贾之家的人而言，比起享乐的源泉，更像是一种责任。
t_en: To one born into a merchant family like me, wealth resembles more of a responsibility than a source of pleasure.

t_ch: 为了不让偌大家业毁于手中，这么多年来，我都在努力与「奢侈」保持距离。
t_en: To prevent the business from going under, I've been working hard to distance myself from luxury all these years.

t_ch: 我不住豪宅、不买豪车，吃穿用度能省就省，家务维修亲力亲为…说实话，我一直过得很辛苦。
t_en: I don't live in a spacious mansion, I don't buy luxury cars, I scrimp and save on what I eat and wear, and I do my own chores... Honestly, I've been living a harsh life.

t_ch: 但现在，我终于能放下责任，用「普通人」的方式来对待钱了。
t_en: But now, I can finally lay down my duty, and treat money in the way that an ordinary person can.

t_ch: 啊…这挥金如土的感觉，可真是该死的甜美啊！
t_en: Ah... it's a blasted good feeling to spend money as if it meant nothing!

t_ch: 自动乐团…真是可悲，没想到匹诺康尼这样高雅的地方会有这么卑劣的技术。
t_en: The automated orchestra... How tragic that an elegant place like Penacony would allow such vulgar technology.

t_ch: 女子怒视着公园里的自动乐团，语气中充斥着鄙夷。
t_en: The lady stares at the park's automated orchestra in seething anger, contempt oozing out of her every breath.

t_ch: 家族将这种东西放在街头，还好意思用音乐代表他们的虔诚…我真为他们感到羞愧。
t_en: The Family dares display these contraptions out on the streets, and they have the cheek to use music to convey their devotion... I feel ashamed on their behalf.

t_ch: 你在生什么气？
t_en: Why are you angry?

t_ch: 自动乐团怎么你了？
t_en: What has the automated orchestra done to you?

t_ch: 朋友，任何一名演奏家都会对这种东西感到愤怒的——他们就是一群亵渎音符的破铜烂铁，只会进行没有灵魂的演奏。
t_en: My friend, any virtuoso would be outraged by this — nothing more than scrap metal desecrating the art of sound and playing soulless music.

t_ch: 如果放任自动乐团被滥用，真人演奏家的生存空间会变得越来越小，这里的音乐艺术将会被污染、破坏、毁灭……
t_en: If automated orchestras become commonplace, there will be less and less room for real musicians to survive, and the art scene here will be contaminated, destroyed, and ruined...

t_ch: 太可怕了！
t_en: That's horrifying!

t_ch: 是的，这是音乐家与乐迷们的噩梦！
t_en: Yes, it's the greatest nightmare for musicians and fans alike!

t_ch: 有点夸大其词…
t_en: That's a bit of an exaggeration...

t_ch: 哼，一点也不夸张，只是你还没有感受到！
t_en: Hmph, it's not an exaggeration! You just can't feel it yet.

t_ch: 如果家族再不重视这个问题，匹诺康尼的演奏家迟早都会出走…梦中属于活物的音乐将会走向末日！
t_en: If The Family does not take this problem seriously, all of Penacony's musicians will leave sooner or later. The music that belongs to living beings in dreams will soon come to an end!

t_ch: 朋友，如果今后不想在这里只能听到如机器般没有灵魂的声响，就请和我一起抵制自动乐团吧！
t_en: My friend, if you do not want to listen to any more of this soulless music played by machines in the future, let's band together and boycott the automated orchestra!

t_ch: 来聊聊天吧。
t_en: Let's chat.

t_ch: 好啊，我喜欢聊天——前提是你足够尊重音乐。
t_en: Sure, I'd love to chat — only if you respect the music.

t_ch: 你是什么人？
t_en: Who are you?

t_ch: 我？我只是一位在宇宙中旅行的演奏家，在各个世界了解当地的音乐形式，再把我自己的音乐带到这个世界。
t_en: Me? I'm just a musician traveling the universe, learning about local music on various worlds and bringing my own music to these worlds.

t_ch: 我曾经很享受这个过程，每个世界都会给我带来新的感触，让我看到不同的艺术表现形式…但不知从何时起，用工具代替乐手的行为开始在宇宙中蔓延。
t_en: I used to enjoy this process, every world brought me new emotions and allowed me to see different forms of artistic expression... But at some point, the replacing of musicians with automated instruments started to spread throughout the universe.

t_ch: 自从发现了这件事以后，我就给了自己一个新的使命——将这些误入歧途的世界拉回正轨！让人们明白——只有活物演奏的音乐才叫音乐！
t_en: Ever since I discovered this, I've made it my life's mission to guide these errant worlds toward the right path! I have to make them understand that music is only music when it's played by the living!

t_ch: 你就这么恨自动乐团？
t_en: You abhor the automated orchestra this much?

t_ch: 当然，如果你不能理解我的想法，就让我给你举个例子吧——你是做什么的？
t_en: Of course, if you don't understand me, allow me to give you an example — what do you do for a living?

t_ch: 我是星穹列车的无名客。
t_en: I'm one of the Nameless on the Astral Express.

t_ch: 无名客…那你想想，假如有人发明了一种智能探测仪，管它们叫「自动无名客」，把你们「开拓」的任务全都抢走了，说不定还要把你踢下列车，你难道不恨它们吗？
t_en: Nameless... Just think about it: If someone invented a smart detector and called it the "Auto-Nameless," it took away all your trailblazing missions and kicked you off the Express, wouldn't you hate it?

t_ch: 我是伸张公义的银河球棒侠。
t_en: I'm the Galactic Baseballer of justice!

t_ch: 球棒侠…那你想想，假如有人创造了一个机器超级英雄，管它叫「自动棒球侠」，把你行侠仗义的风头全都抢走了，还盗用你的形象，你难道不恨它吗？
t_en: Baseballer... Just think about it: If someone invented a robot superhero and called it the "Auto-Baseballer," it stole your image and all your glory, wouldn't you hate it?

t_ch: 我是无业游民。
t_en: I'm unemployed.

t_ch: 呃…那你想想，假如有人发明了一种仿生机器，管它们叫「自动无业游民」，把你能领的补助、睡觉的桥洞、要翻的垃圾桶全抢走了，你难道不恨它们吗？
t_en: Uh... Well, just think about it: If someone invented an android and called it the "Auto-unemployed," it took away all your subsidies and your sleeping spots and snatched away all your trashcans, wouldn't you hate it?

t_ch: 这么说你就能懂了吧？这些自动机器看似是在提供便利，实则是在迫害它们身为有机生命的同行，是在扼杀各个行业的未来！
t_en: Do you understand what I'm saying now? These automatic tools may seem convenient, but they're actually cannibalizing their organic counterparts, killing the future of every industry!

t_ch: 下次再聊…
t_en: Let's talk next time...

t_ch: 再见。
t_en: Thank you.

t_ch: 再见，朋友，小心别让自动乐团破坏了你的审美。
t_en: Goodbye, my friend, be careful not to let the automated orchestra ruin your artistic taste.

t_ch: 布罗娜紧皱眉头，怨恨与怒火充盈在她的眼中——如果不赶快劝劝她，下一秒她可能就会把面前的自动乐团砸个稀巴烂。
t_en: Brona frowns, resentment and hatred filling her eyes — if you don't quickly persuade her, she'll likely smash the automated orchestra in front of her into smithereens in the next second.

t_ch: 这毫无灵魂的杂音…真是让人心烦意乱！我再也不能忍受这些的冰冷的魔鬼在街头啸叫了！
t_en: This soulless cacophony... It's getting on my nerves! I can't stand these cold devils howling down the streets any longer!

t_ch: 冷静点！
t_en: Calm down!

t_ch: 别冲动！
t_en: Don't be rash!

t_ch: 朋友，你不知道这些东西会伤害多少人吗？他们不是乐器，是武器！是杀害艺术家的武器！
t_en: My friend, do you grasp the harm these things can bring to people? They're not musical instruments, they're weapons! Weapons that kill artists!

t_ch: 自动乐团、托腔机巧、义体奏乐宏…这些东西让我们乐手数年乃至数十年、数百年的勤学苦练都失去了意义！
t_en: The automated orchestra, musical accompaniment ingenia, artificial music creation... These are the things that render the years, decades, and even centuries of hard work by us musicians utterly meaningless!

t_ch: 如今有多少演奏家失去了听众的尊重，又有多少喜欢音乐的人因为这些歪门邪道而放弃学习乐器！长此以往，音乐将亡！
t_en: Countless musicians have lost the respect of their fans, and many aficionados have given up on learning their instruments because of these crooked practices! Over time, music will wither and die!

t_ch: 听着，那边的指挥家，停下你罪恶的指挥棒！那是屠杀艺术的屠刀！
t_en: Listen up, conductor! Lower your evil baton! That is the executioner's axe upon all art!

t_ch: 她不理会你的劝说，冲着自动乐团的指挥家大喊大叫——但对方沉浸在自动奏响的音乐之中，也不理会她的反抗。
t_en: She disregards your advice and yells at the conductor of the automated orchestra — the conductor, immersed in music, ignores her resistance.

t_ch: 布罗娜看着自动乐团，嘴角颤动起来——她开始发出不屑的嗤笑。
t_en: Brona looks at the automated orchestra, the corners of her mouth twitching as she exudes a scornful sneer.

t_ch: 呵呵…呵呵呵，如此空洞的演奏，没有一丝情感的流露，没有一丁点独创的节奏。
t_en: Heheh... Heheheh, such a hollow performance, devoid of any emotion, not a trace of personal interpretation of rhythm...

t_ch: 是我想得太多了，区区物件的拙劣模仿，怎么能比得上人类用心奏响的音乐呢？
t_en: I'm overthinking it — how can music made by a mere object compare to music made from the heart?

t_ch: 真正的演奏艺术，绝不是用乐器的振动发出乐谱上的声音这么简单…那是乐器、旋律、乐手一同合作的艺术品，蕴含着一个音乐人对艺术的感悟和他的灵魂！
t_en: The art of musical performances goes beyond merely using the instrument and translating the notes from a sheet of music... It involves the harmonious collaboration of the instrument, melody, and musician, working together to craft a masterpiece. This encompasses the interpretation and soul of the musician!

t_ch: 朋友，你应该能听出来吧？自动乐团的音乐完全无法跟真人演奏相比！
t_en: My friend, can't you hear it? The music from an automated orchestra pales in comparison to the music produced by a living, breathing human!

t_ch: 我听不出来…
t_en: I can't tell...

t_ch: 你…看来你并不是懂得欣赏音乐艺术，可惜。
t_en: You... It appears that you don't seem to appreciate the essence of musical artistry, what a pity.

t_ch: 呵呵，但也无所谓，我们演奏家并不是为你这种人工作的——我们的演奏只为满足那些尊重音乐的人，还有取悦我们自己的灵魂！
t_en: Heheh, but it doesn't matter. We musicians don't play for people like you — we only perform for those who appreciate music, and to sate the yearnings of love that our souls crave!

t_ch: 有一说一，确实。
t_en: You do make sense, indeed.

t_ch: 太好了，你这样懂得尊重音乐的人，才是真正值得我们倾注心血演奏的听众。
t_en: Fantastic! People like you who truly appreciate music are the ones we find deserving of our performances into which we pour our hearts and souls.

t_ch: 至于自动乐团的演奏…谁愿意听就去听吧！那些人永远无法理解真正的艺术！而一个伟大的音乐家也绝不会被这种「技术」轻易打败！
t_en: As for the performances by the automated orchestra... Whoever wants to listen can go ahead! Those people will never understand true art! A great musician will never be discouraged by such a travesty on art!

t_ch: 朋友，这些苜蓿币给你——答应我，拿它们去看场音乐会吧，台上全是活人的那种。
t_en: My friend, take these Alfalfa credits — just promise me, take these coins and go watch a performance where music is played by the living!

t_ch: 去聆听吧，去感受吧，去了解艺术真正应有的样子吧！
t_en: Hear it, feel it, and experience art as it should be!

t_ch: 区区物件的拙劣模仿，怎么能比得上人类用心奏响的音乐呢？
t_en: How can a crude imitation compare to music made from the heart?

t_ch: 真正的演奏艺术，是乐器、旋律、乐手一同合作的艺术品，蕴含着一个音乐人对艺术的感悟和他的灵魂！
t_en: The essence of true performance art lies in the harmonious collaboration of musical instruments, melodies, and musicians — a fusion that encompasses the musician's interpretation of art and their soul.

t_ch: 布罗娜直勾勾地望着自动乐团，眼眸逐渐失神——她似乎被起舞的乐器夺走了灵魂。
t_en: Brona stares at the automated orchestra, her eyes slowly losing focus — her soul seems to have been hooked by the melodious instruments.

t_ch: 唉，仔细想想…我所做的一切应该都是徒劳啊……
t_en: Alas, upon reflection... It seems that everything I've done has been in vain...
